own_review_points: IcebergAvroFileWriter constructor calls validateCompression(AVRO, ...) — redundant with upstream validation in createTableProperties/setTableProperties (visible in window)
validateCompression run on the read/metadata path would fail DESCRIBE/SHOW CREATE for tables created by other engines with an incompatible codec — should be write-path only (per v8; past truncation)
Stored codec representation changes case (.name() uppercase) vs prior lowercase — cross-engine readers may differ (per v8; past truncation)
IcebergMetadata.setTableProperties: the newFileFormat/oldFileFormat handling around line 2502 looks suspect (human flags it as a likely bug; v8 instead asserts it is correct)
Static-import AVRO consistently across the writer files (visible: AVRO static import is being added)
notes: Smallest, most checkable PR. v8's redundant-AVRO-validation point lands on code visible in the window and is accurate (good). The other six points target IcebergUtil/IcebergMetadata/test code past the 7000-char truncation and are unverifiable, though several (read-path validation breaking foreign tables, codec case change) would be strong if accurate. v8 misses all three human-reference points: it actively dismisses the newFileFormat==oldFileFormat line the maintainer flags as a bug, omits the 'static import AVRO consistently' nit despite that import being visible, and omits the commit-message fix. Net -2.
